codeReview.summary: The fix commit addresses several reported points (dashboard pane rename, removeTool active-session cleanup path, and integration event wiring hooks), but three high-confidence correctness gaps remain: platform tools are still not wired onto MCP sessions at runtime, dynamic tool removal is incomplete for tools registered during session creation, and the adapter API wiring still degrades to mock-success behavior in default installs because required SDK dependencies are not present in package.json.

issues.description: PlatformToolsManager is instantiated and registry load/unload events are subscribed, but no McpServer instances are ever registered via PlatformToolsManager.addServer(). Because PlatformToolsManager.registerIntegrationTools() only registers tools on its internal servers set, integration lifecycle events still do not make platform tools available in active MCP sessions at runtime.

removeTool() relies on per-session dynamicToolHandles, but dynamic tools registered during createMcpServer() (for sessions created after addTool()) are not stored in dynamicToolHandles. As a result, removeTool() will fail to deregister those tools from such active sessions, leaving stale tools in tools/list/tools/call despite removal from the map.

The fix claims real X/Twitter, Slack, and Email API wiring, but package.json does not include twitter-api-v2, @slack/bolt, nodemailer, or imap-simple. Current adapters silently fall back to connected=true with null clients and 'would be sent/posted' success responses (e.g., x-twitter.ts:137, slack.ts:141, email.ts:225), so default project installs still do not provide real API behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission features use skillName 'worker', but repo-level skills are absent, and prior transcripts repeatedly show 'Skill "worker" not found' deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json entries use "skillName": "worker"; /Users/teodorwaltervido/Desktop/service/.factory/skills is empty; worker transcript skeletons for f3/f4 include Skill tool errors.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission-local transcript index is incomplete for this reviewed fix session, requiring lookup in global session logs to review transcript skeleton.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl in mission has 14 entries and no row for workerSessionId d9613a1f-7b3b-48fb-be25-f982b6c7621c; skeleton was retrieved from /Users/teodorwaltervido/.factory/sessions/-Users-teodorwaltervido/d9613a1f-7b3b-48fb-be25-f982b6c7621c.jsonl.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Reviewer guidance references .factory/library/architecture.md for boundary checks, but repository library lacks that file.

summary: Review failed. The fix commit meaningfully advances the prior failures, but it does not yet fully satisfy the original blocking requirements: platform tool lifecycle remains functionally unhooked to MCP sessions, dynamic tool removal is incomplete for session-init registrations, and real adapter behavior is not guaranteed in default installs due to missing runtime SDK dependencies and mock-success fallbacks.
